items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: The council held a study session on April 14 to examine the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines planned infrastructure and development projects. This is worth watching to see what physical improvements and investments Mountain View is prioritizing.

items.headline: Sunnyvale moves to strengthen tenant protections
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance amending the Residential Tenant Protections Program on Tuesday, April 14th. This could expand protections for renters in Sunnyvale, though specific changes weren't detailed in the agenda.

items.headline: City releases Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan
items.summary: San José City Council received a status report on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9, outlining improvements to library services and physical locations. This plan could affect where residents access library resources and what services are available at local branches.

items.headline: Downtown historic building gets bonus floor space for upgrades
items.summary: The city is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area if the building undergoes historic and seismic rehabilitation. This could reshape a downtown property.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road
items.summary: A proposal to rezone and redevelop property at 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road into an 8-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and commercial space is under review. This represents significant new housing and a major change to that area's character.

items.headline: Development project at 333 Franklin Street hearing postponed
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street has been cancelled and rescheduled to April 22, 2026. Residents interested in this project should watch for a new notice with the updated hearing date.

items.headline: City awards contract for Bascom Avenue traffic signal improvements
items.summary: The City Council approved a third amendment to improve traffic signals at the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail crossing. This project addresses safety at a major intersection and could improve traffic flow for South Bay commuters.

items.headline: City Council updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The council reviewed Phase 2 of Cupertino's Active Transportation Plan, including revised scoring criteria and edits. This plan will guide future investments in biking, walking, and transit infrastructure across the city.
